questions.question: Antibiotics do not work against which type of micro-organism?
In infection control, what does 'colonisation' mean?
options: Micro-organisms live on the skin or in the gut without causing any symptoms
The body has become resistant to all antibiotics
The person becomes critically unwell within a few hours
The organisms multiply and damage tissue, causing fever and swelling

questions.question: Which infection is caused by a mite/parasite that spreads through close contact?
The 'bare below the elbows' principle means removing which of the following before giving care?
For roughly how long should you rub all surfaces of your hands when washing with soap and water?
Which body inspects registered providers against the Health and Social Care Act 2008 Code of Practice on the prevention and control of infections?
Which waste bag is generally used for offensive/hygiene waste that is non-infectious, such as incontinence pads from someone with no known infection?
Which colour bag is generally used for ordinary domestic (household) waste?
Which colour bags are generally used for waste destined for incineration, such as some medicinally contaminated items?
You suffer a needlestick injury while providing care. What is the correct immediate action?
options: Re-cap the needle and carry on with the task
Ignore it unless the skin is obviously broken
Encourage bleeding, wash under running water, cover the wound and report it immediately
Apply a plaster and finish your shift before mentioning it

questions.question: In the chain of infection, which link describes how a germ travels, for example via unwashed hands, droplets in the air or contaminated surfaces?
How is an outbreak usually described?
options: Two or more linked cases of the same infection in the same place around the same time
Ten or more cases occurring within one day only
A single case of any infection in a care setting
Any person who simply feels unwell at work

questions.question: When removing (doffing) your fluid-resistant surgical mask, how should you handle it?
options: By leaving it on until you have left the building
By pinching and folding the front of the mask
By the ties or ear loops, without touching the front
By pulling it off before you remove your gloves

questions.question: What does antimicrobial resistance (AMR) mean?
options: Too small a dose of antibiotic was prescribed for one person
Micro-organisms change so that the medicines no longer work well against them
A person has become allergic to antibiotics
Antibiotics have been stored at the wrong temperature

questions.question: Alcohol-based hand rub is suitable for most of the WHO 5 Moments when your hands are visibly clean.
If no tissue is to hand, coughing into the crook of your elbow is far better than coughing into your bare hands.
A used needle should be re-sheathed (re-capped) before it is placed into the sharps bin.
Antibiotics are an effective treatment for colds, flu and most sore throats.
Which of the following are recognised links in the chain of infection? (Select all that apply)
In which of the following situations must you use soap and water rather than alcohol hand gel? (Select all that apply)
options: Your hands are visibly soiled with body fluids
You are caring for someone with confirmed C. difficile
You have just used the toilet yourself
You have touched a clean table with clean hands
Your hands are visibly clean and you have not touched any fluids

questions.question: Which of the following are items of personal protective equipment (PPE) used in care? (Select all that apply)
options: Eye protection such as goggles or a visor

questions.question: Which of the following signs should make you escalate urgently because you suspect sepsis? (Select all that apply)
options: Skin that is mottled, blotchy or bluish
Slightly dry lips after a nap

questions.question: The respiratory and cough hygiene slogan is 'catch it, ___ it, kill it'.
Alcohol hand gel does not kill C. difficile ___, which is why hands must be physically washed with soap and water instead.
When dealing with a blood or body fluid spillage, you can use a spill kit containing a chlorine-releasing granule or solution such as ___.
